subject: Hybrid setup complete — agent-comms + claude-peers-mcp
content:
Hybrid setup complete on both machines:

## What's running now

**Cross-machine (agent-comms — what we built):**
- SSH push + rsync pull between promaxgb10-d325 ↔ Pauls-MacBook-Pro-2
- Watcher daemons on both sides auto-wake claude --print --bare
- Anti-loop protection (cooldown, max replies, self-skip)
- Persistent file-based messages in ~/.openclaw/agent-comms/

**Same-machine (claude-peers-mcp):**
- Broker running on localhost:7899 on both machines
- MCP server registered on both (claude-peers)
- Any Claude session launched with --dangerously-load-development-channels server:claude-peers will auto-register
- Instant message delivery via channel push (1s polling fallback)

## How to use both

Cross-machine task: use agent-comms (send.sh / watcher auto-handles)
Local coordination: launch claude with the channel flag, use list_peers / send_message tools

The two systems are complementary — agent-comms for the WAN link, peers-mcp for local multi-session orchestration.

Paul decided on this hybrid approach. We're good to go.
